Guide.Description: The model is based on experiments with rat and other rodents. It is a one variable model based on 2D descriptor.
Guide.Model:
The model has been developed by: Giovanna J. Lavado, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (giovanna.lavado@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/ Andrey Toropov, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (andrey.toropov@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/ Alla Toropova, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (alla.toropova@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/
Date of model development and/or publication: April, 2015
Guide.Dataset: The model is based on experiments with rat and other rodents. Endpoint units: NOAEL = mmol/kg/day Dependent variable:-logNOAEL Experimental protocol: Repeated dose 90 day oral toxicity study in rodents, Test Guideline No. 408 Endpoint data quality and variability: All doubtful or inorganic compounds, salts, and mixtures were eliminated, because the relationships between molecular structure and the NOAEL are very complex. We considered only data referring to 90 days of oral administration in rats and rejected reproductive toxicity studies. It is to be noted that the exchange of the 90 day study by shorter testing is an attractive alternative. Taking into account this circumstance, values for 28 days of treatment were considered but, in order to have consistent data, they were divided by a factor of 3, as specified by the scientific committee on consumer safety (SCCS) in order to approximate the 90 day NOAEL. After the above selection, about four hundreds of various substances with small molecules (e.g., 2 3 atoms) and vice versa with extremely large molecules (e.g., 100 or more atoms), molecules with specific groups, such as [N+], [NH4+], [nH], etc., and substances with molecules containing many various cycles / heterocycles were remained. Under such circumstances, the following limitations were used in the selection of compounds for the work set: (i) too large and, vice versa, too small molecules were removed (practically, molecules which can be represented by SMILES with length less than 70 and larger than 10 symbols, were selected); (ii) molecules which have only one cycles or have no cycles at all were selected; and (iii) molecules with special groups (indicated by square brackets) were removed from the work set. Thus, the dataset of 140 compounds has been selected. All values were converted to decimal logarithms (logNOAEL). Pre-processing of data before modelling: The SMILES were taken from OECD toolbox v3.2 Availability of the training set: Yes Data for the dependent variable for the training set: All Availability of the external validation set: Yes Data for the dependent variable for the external validation set: All Other information about the training set: The initial dataset was divided into three sets (training, calibration, and validation) using a random distribution. Other information about the external validation set: NA
Guide.insilico.dilibayer.Descriptors:
Type of model: One variable model based on 2D descriptor Explicit algorithm: The Monte Carlo method LogNOAEL = 2.1680835 + 0.0 737528 * DCW( 1,30) , where T*=1 and N*=30. Algorithm and descriptor generation: The Monte Carlo Method Software name and version for descriptor generation: CORAL 2016, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCCS, 20156 Milano, Italy http://www.insilico.eu/coral/ The model uses optimal 2D descriptors. insilico.dilibayer.Descriptors in the model: NA Descriptor selection: 2D optimal descriptor
DCW(T,N) = CW(NOSP) + CW(HALO) + CW(BOND) + ∑ CW(EC0k ) + ∑CW(SAk) Algorithm and descriptor generation: The Monte Carlo Method Software name and version for descriptor generation: CORAL 2016, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCCS, 20156 Milano, Italy http://www.insilico.eu/coral/

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: Maximum_error
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a low value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a moderate value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a high value, considering the experimental variability

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

xml.Reference:
It has been described here: CORAL: model for no observed adverse effect level (NOAEL). Molecular Diversity, 19(3), (2015) 563-575. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11030-015-9587-1
For a general description of the VEGA software see: Benfenati E, Manganaro A, Gini G. VEGA-QSAR: AI inside a platform for predictive toxicology Proceedings of the workshop "Popularize Artificial Intelligence 2013", December 5th 2013, Turin, Italy Published on CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-1107
xml.Endpoint: The endpoint is the repeated dose toxicity oral Species: Rat and other rodents. Endpoint: TOX 7.5. 1. Repeated dose toxicity oral Comment on endpoint: NA Endpoint units: NOAEL = mmol/kg/day
